Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARTA MCDOWELL, CARRI LUCAS, KIMBERLY REYNOLDS, LYNN REILLY, and, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Phyllis Elise Andelin, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Lisa Berkowitz, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3825 appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment denied. Motion for Summary adjudication granted in part & denied in part. (For complete minutes for this motion, see minutes in word document. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to December 14, 2009, per ex parte Order of 12/7/09. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order Regarding Pltf'S Deposition; Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Answers In Depostion
Off Calendar Aug-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Lisa Berkowitz, Deputy City Attorney, for defendant and moving party: Carri Lucas, Kimberly Reynods, Lynn Reilly and City and County of San Francisco
Phyllis E. Andelin, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Lori A. Dutra
After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted.
Defendants Marta Mcdowell, Carri Lucas, Kimberly Reynods, Lynn Reilly and City and County of San Francisco's Motion to compel and for sanctions granted, as follows:
1. Motion to compel granted; counsel for plaintiff has agreed that plaintiff will appear for deposition;
a. Plaintiff shall appear for further deposition within thirty days of notice of entry of this order, on a date to be agreed upon by counsel for the parties, or as designated by counsel for defendants;
b. Unless the parties otherwise agree, the deposition shall take place in Courtroom 610, at the Civic Center Courthouse--beginning at 1:30 p.m., and ending not later than 4:30 p.m.-in one or more sessions as may be required to complete the deposition.
2. Request for sanctions denied without prejudice, pending the deposition of plaintiff.
Deposition to proceed on August 21 and August 24, if needed.
Defendant may make general inquiry concerning to begining of plaintiff's correct relationship; its duration, plaintiff disclosure of the relationship to her co-workers, etc.Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
